verified.notes: Income limits for Hawaii MSPs (QMB, SLMB, QI-1) are based on 135% of Federal Poverty Level per 2026 HHS guidelines. Asset limits and official application phone number not found in provided search results. Search results show conflicting asset limit information from non-current sources (2021 data showing $9,430 individual/$14,130 couple for QMB, and older federal standards). No authoritative 2026 asset limits or official state phone number retrieved. Recommend contacting Hawaii Department of Human Services or Medicare.gov directly for current asset limits and application phone number.

verified.notes: Income limit is 200% of Federal Poverty Guidelines (not fixed dollar amount in sources provided). Monthly income limits vary by household size and are set annually by federal poverty guidelines; specific dollar amounts for household sizes 1 and 2 not provided in retrieved sources. No age limit found. No asset limits found in sources. Phone number is for Honolulu Community Action Program (HCAP), primary WAP administrator for Oahu; Hawaii County Economic Opportunity Council (HCEOC) serves Big Island with different contact info. State administrator is Hawaii Office of Community Services (OCS) at labor.hawaii.gov.

programs.programName: Home-Delivered Meals (via Kūpuna Aging in Place grantees)

verified.notes: Program is Kūpuna Aging in Place (KAP), a multi-year grant program administered by Hawaii Community Foundation supporting organizations that provide home-delivered meals and other services. Age requirement of 65+ found in official HCF source. Income guidelines exist (program targets low- to moderate-income kūpuna) but specific thresholds not provided in available sources. No official phone number for direct application found; applications are submitted online through HCF Grants Portal. Current funding cycle is 2026-2029 with application deadline of March 20, 2026 (already passed as of query date). No .gov source available; primary source is Hawaii Community Foundation official website.
